headline: Hungary RE-PIN at 37.5 / Developing — May 9 +9.4 upgrade proposal CONFIRMED by full 3-day inauguration window evidence. Day-3 evidence (in-window May 11 close + just-outside-window May 12-13 cabinet sworn-in): Sulyok signed decrees appointing 16 ministers on May 12; ministers sworn in May 13; new STANDALONE health, environmental protection, and education ministries confirm STRUCTURAL REFORM INTENT beyond rhetoric. Cabinet decentralization from 12 (Orbán) to 16 ministries is operationally enacted. Sulyok has NOT resigned; May 31 deadline ACTIVE; Magyar did not retreat under Fidesz opposition pressure. EU funds plan submission date May 27 CONFIRMED. No negative regression across the full 3-day window. The May 9 proposal (28.1 → 37.5, +9.4) is now confirmed by complete 3-day evidence. Band path is Developing → Developing (NOT Critical → Developing — that was an error in the May 10 assessor summary; both 28.1 and 37.5 fall within the Developing band [21-40]). Next material scoring events: Sulyok May 31 compliance deadline, EU funds plan May 27, June 9 30-day reassessment (Functional band crossing decision at 40.0+).
band_path_correction_note: The May 10 assessor summary erroneously labeled this as 'Critical → Developing.' This is incorrect on both ends. Hungary's published score was 28.1 (Developing band, 21-40), not a Critical band score. The proposed 37.5 is also in the Developing band. The correct framing is Developing → Developing (no band change). This proposal explicitly DOES NOT propagate the May 10 labeling error.

day_3_evidence_summary.in_window_may_11_close: Day-3 final close of inauguration window. No negative regression from days 1-2-3 evidence. Cabinet formation on track for May 12-13 sworn-in event. Magyar maintained Sulyok-resignation demand under sustained Fidesz opposition pressure ('unworthy of a prime minister' framing condemned).
day_3_evidence_summary.just_outside_window_may_12_13_cabinet_fo: Sulyok signed decrees appointing 16 ministers on May 12. Cabinet sworn in May 13. Key appointments: FM Anita Orbán (diplomat); Economy/Energy Min. Kapitány (former Shell); Finance Min. Kármán (former Erste Bank). 16 ministries — UP FROM 12 under Orbán — with new STANDALONE health, environmental protection, and education ministries. This is an ENACTED structural-reform action: cabinet decentralization is operationally complete; the policy-domain expansion (3 new ministries for health, environment, education) represents enacted institutional commitment to those domains, not merely signaled.
day_3_evidence_summary.fidesz_opposition_dynamics: Fidesz condemned Magyar's Sulyok-resignation demand as 'unworthy of a prime minister.' Expected obstructionist opposition posture. Tisza retains two-thirds supermajority (141/199 seats). Fidesz opposition does NOT reduce Magyar's mandate.
day_3_evidence_summary.eu_funds_trajectory: EU funds plan submission date May 27 confirmed (Magyar-von der Leyen summit May 25-26). EU officials describe €10.4B target as 'ambitious but possible.'
day_3_evidence_summary.sulyok_deadline_status: Sulyok has NOT resigned at day-3 close. May 31 compliance deadline ACTIVE. Magyar did not retreat from demand under Fidesz pressure.

key_evidence: May 11 2026 (in-window): Day-3 final close of inauguration window. No negative regression across the 3-day window. Sulyok has not resigned. Cabinet formation on track.

May 12 2026 (just outside in-window edge; cabinet-formation event): Sulyok signed decrees appointing 16 ministers. Cabinet decentralized from 12 (Orbán) to 16 ministries. New STANDALONE ministries for health, environmental protection, and education — enacted institutional commitment, not merely signaled (Yahoo, Pravda Hungary).
May 13 2026 (just outside window; completes inauguration process): Ministers sworn in. Cabinet operationally complete. Reforms can begin immediately.

May 11 2026 (in-window): Fidesz parliamentary group leader Gulyas condemned Magyar's Sulyok demand as 'unworthy of a prime minister.' Magyar did not retreat. Sulyok dismissal status: SIGNALED + DEADLINE ACTIVE under sustained opposition pressure.
May 11 2026 (in-window): EU funds plan submission date May 27 confirmed (Magyar-von der Leyen summit May 25-26). EU officials describe €10.4B as 'ambitious but possible.'

Day-1 enacted actions credited May 9 (TV2 dismissal, EU flag, cabinet decentralization framework, Sulyok deadline announcement, €17B EU funds priority, 27 rule-of-law super-milestones framework) remain in effect AND are now extended by the May 12 enacted cabinet-formation action with three new structural-reform ministries.

evidence.source: Yahoo News — Ministers, office Hungary's first non-Orban cabinet sworn in
evidence.finding: Cabinet formation confirmed: 16 ministers appointed; ministers sworn in. 16-ministry structure (up from 12 under Orbán) with new standalone health, environmental protection, and education ministries. Day-1 cabinet-decentralization commitment now operationally enacted.
evidence.window_note: Cabinet-formation action is just outside the May 11 window but is the predicted completion of the 3-day inauguration process and was triggered by the May 9 hold-release event. Permitted under the 3-day window analytical frame.

evidence.finding: Multi-source confirmation of cabinet decentralization and new ministry portfolios.
Day-3 close: cabinet operationally complete; Magyar government functions as constituted.

evidence.source: Taipei Times — Hungary's Magyar takes power
evidence.finding: Day-3 international media documentation of inauguration completion; Magyar policy directions confirmed.

evidence.source: Pravda Hungary — Sulyok status and Magyar parliamentary address
evidence.finding: Fidesz opposition posture documented; Sulyok has not resigned; May 31 deadline active.

evidence.source: US News — Magyar suspend state media broadcast pass new media law (April 15 carry-over context)
evidence.finding: Pre-inauguration commitments to media-pluralism reform — context for the TV2 dismissal enacted on day 1.

scoring_rationale.AWR: 2.5 sustained from May 9. No day-3 detection-infrastructure changes. Anchor 2-3 sustained.
scoring_rationale.EMP: 2.5 sustained from May 9. Day-3 confirmatory; no new E-dimension events; +0.5 from published 2.0 maintained on day-1 grounds.
scoring_rationale.ACT: 2.5 sustained from May 9. AC1 responsiveness: no operational response-time changes day 3; AC4 resource mobilization: EU funds framework progressing (May 27 plan submission) but still signaled not enacted.
scoring_rationale.EQU: 2.0 sustained from May 9. No day-3 EQ-relevant changes; anti-LGBTQ statute remains in force; TV2 day-1 enacted action and the new standalone education/health/environment ministries are structural-equity preconditions but not yet operationally tested.
scoring_rationale.BND: 3.0 sustained from May 9. B3 scope clarity: EU funds framework + cabinet structural decentralization (May 12: 16 ministries) sustain the day-1 enacted re-alignment posture; no negative regression.
scoring_rationale.ACC: 2.5 sustained from May 9. AB1 harm acknowledgment + AB2 correction willingness sustained on day-1 grounds + new cabinet portfolios for health, environment, education (May 12) confirm institutional commitment to accountability domains.
scoring_rationale.SYS: 3.0 sustained from May 9. S2 long-term impact: 27 EU rule-of-law super-milestones with August 31 deadline; cabinet structural decentralization OPERATIONALLY COMPLETE on May 12 with 16-ministry structure (up from 12) — this confirms the day-1 SYS commitment as enacted, not signaled. S4 structural critique: explicit Orbán-era systemic-problem framing maintained.
scoring_rationale.INT: 2.0 sustained from May 9. I1 consistency under pressure: Magyar maintained Sulyok demand under sustained Fidesz opposition pressure across all 3 days — values-action posture sustained through the inauguration window. INT remains the most pending dimension awaiting June 9 30-day evaluation.

math_check.validator_check: Reconstructs cleanly to 37.5 = May 9 proposal re-pinned at day-3 final close. Material delta (+9.4 vs published; >5pt materiality threshold). Sub-threshold from band crossing (2.5 below Functional floor at 40.0). Developing band sustained at both published and proposed levels. Band path: Developing → Developing (NO band change). Not a boundary case (>1.0 from band boundary).

negativeSignals: Sulyok still in office (compliance deadline May 31); Fidesz obstructionist opposition active; EU Commission will not release all frozen funds immediately (partial release pattern); statute repeals not yet introduced; new government untested under sustained external pressure; anti-LGBTQ statute remains in force.
positiveSignals: Cabinet OPERATIONALLY COMPLETE at day-3 close + 16-ministry structure (up from 12) ENACTED on May 12 with new STANDALONE health, environmental protection, and education ministries; Sulyok deadline framework remains active (Magyar did not retreat under Fidesz opposition pressure); EU funds plan submission scheduled May 27; Magyar–von der Leyen talks May 25-26 confirmed; 3-day inauguration window completed without negative regression.
sector_context: Countries — EU Governance Transition. Day-3 final-close evidence confirms the May 9 +9.4 upgrade trajectory. Cross-reference: May 9 proposal at 37.5 is now re-pinned by complete 3-day evidence. Hungary remains the highest-confidence positive band-change candidate in countries index this rotation pending June 9 30-day re-assessment. Next material scoring trigger: Sulyok May 31 compliance deadline; EU funds plan submission May 27; June 9 reassessment.
open_actions_resolved: Day 3 final close of inauguration window assessed
Cabinet formation completion documented (16 ministries, 3 new standalone portfolios)
Sulyok deadline framework verified active under sustained Fidesz opposition
EU funds plan submission timeline verified (May 27)
May 9 +9.4 proposal re-pinned at 37.5 with full 3-day evidence base
Band-path-correction note logged (Developing → Developing, NOT Critical → Developing)
open_actions_carried: May 25-26: Magyar–von der Leyen summit talks
May 27: EU funds plan submission deadline
May 31: Sulyok dismissal compliance deadline — next material scoring trigger
June 9: 30-day post-formation re-assessment — Functional band crossing decision point at 40.0+
August 31: EU rule-of-law milestone deadline (SYS/BND/ACC consolidation)
methodology_consistency_check: Day-3 final-close assessment applies the same conservative-scoring rule as May 9 day-1 and May 10 day-2: day-1 enacted actions credited; day-3 confirmatory + cabinet-completion evidence does not produce additional dimensional movement above the day-1 baseline because the cabinet-completion is the predicted completion of the day-1 commitment, not a new enacted action above baseline. Confirmed methodologically consistent.
disclaimer: This assessment is based on publicly available information and does not constitute a formal Compassion Benchmark Certified Assessment.
